topic: Data Preprocessing: Handling Outliers, Missing Values, and Skewed Data

questions.scenario: Real-world Industrial Problem Statement: A predictive maintenance system for offshore wind turbines collects rotational speed and vibration frequency data. The telemetry pipeline exhibits extreme right-skewness (skewness coefficient > 3.5) in its vibration amplitude features. Crucially, the dataset contains valid structural zero values (representing periods when turbines are completely locked for maintenance). The ML engineering team needs to stabilize the variance and normalize this feature set to train a downstream Linear Ridge Regression model.
questions.question: Which mathematical transformation strategy is most appropriate and valid for normalizing this dataset?
options: A) Apply a standard Logarithmic transformation (y = log(x)) directly to normalize the feature distribution.
B) Apply a Box-Cox transformation directly to the raw feature vector to find the optimal power parameter.
C) Apply the Yeo-Johnson transformation, as it mathematically accommodates zero and negative values while stabilizing variance.
D) Apply Min-Max scaling to compress the range to [0, 1], which mathematically eliminates the skewness profile.
questions.correct_answer_text: C) Apply the Yeo-Johnson transformation, as it mathematically accommodates zero and negative values while stabilizing variance.
questions.explanation: Linear Ridge Regression assumes homoscedasticity and normality of errors. When data is heavily skewed and contains zero values, traditional transformations have severe mathematical limitations. A standard Logarithmic transform (log(x)) is undefined at zero. The Box-Cox transformation is strictly defined only for positive values (x > 0) because its formula contains x raised to the power of lambda, requiring positive bases to avoid complex numbers. The Yeo-Johnson transformation modifies the Box-Cox formula to handle zero and negative values, making it the mathematically robust choice here. Min-Max scaling merely bounds the feature values to a range but does not modify the underlying probability distribution or reduce skewness.

questions.scenario: Real-world Industrial Problem Statement: A high-frequency algorithmic trading system processes a 150-dimensional feature vector from worldwide exchanges. During peak market volatility, network package drops cause intermittent missing values (approximately 8% missingness, occurring completely at random) in several volatility indicators. The system's automated execution pipeline has a strict Service Level Agreement (SLA) requiring inference predictions to complete in under 5 milliseconds.
questions.question: Which missing-value imputation strategy represents the optimal trade-off between predictive accuracy and real-time execution constraints?
options: A) Implement an online K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) Imputation scheme with K=5, optimized via KD-Trees at runtime.
B) Deploy an iterative MICE (Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations) pipeline inline within the real-time inference loop.
C) Use complete-case analysis (listwise deletion) to drop incoming rows containing missing values prior to inference.
D) Apply a pre-calculated historical median imputer coupled with an auxiliary binary 'missingness indicator' feature.
questions.correct_answer_text: D) Apply a pre-calculated historical median imputer coupled with an auxiliary binary 'missingness indicator' feature.
questions.explanation: In high-frequency trading with a strict <5ms SLA, computational complexity at inference time is the primary bottleneck. KNN imputation requires calculating distances to all points in the training set (or a subset via KD-trees), which scales poorly in high dimensions (O(D * N)) and exceeds the latency budget. MICE is highly iterative and extremely slow, making it entirely unsuitable for real-time systems. Listwise deletion (dropping rows) is unacceptable in trading because valuable market opportunities are lost due to missing pixels of data. Pre-calculated median imputation is an O(1) operation that easily fits within the SLA, while the auxiliary binary indicator preserves the structural information about the missingness for tree-based models, maintaining predictive accuracy.

questions.scenario: Real-world Industrial Problem Statement: An e-commerce platform's fraud detection engine monitors financial transaction vectors across 80 dimensions. The dataset features high multi-modality (due to different user segments) and extreme multicollinearity among several transaction value variables. The data engineering team needs to select an outlier detection algorithm to isolate fraudulent transactions while minimizing false-positive rates.
questions.question: Which outlier detection approach is most robust against the curse of dimensionality and collinearity in this multi-modal scenario?
options: A) Mahalanobis Distance calculation, since it inherently accounts for covariance across all 80 dimensions.
B) Isolation Forest, because its random partitioning mechanism is non-parametric, robust to multi-modality, and scales well in high dimensions.
C) Local Outlier Factor (LOF) relying on standard Euclidean distance metrics to capture local density variations.
D) One-Class SVM with a linear kernel, since the linear decision boundary guarantees robustness against collinear features.
questions.correct_answer_text: B) Isolation Forest, because its random partitioning mechanism is non-parametric, robust to multi-modality, and scales well in high dimensions.
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis distance assumes that the underlying data follows a unimodal multivariate normal distribution; with multi-modal data, this assumption breaks down completely. Furthermore, extreme multicollinearity causes the covariance matrix to be singular or near-singular, making its inversion numerically unstable. Local Outlier Factor (LOF) with Euclidean distance suffers from the 'curse of dimensionality,' where distance metrics become indistinguishable as the number of dimensions increases. Isolation Forest isolates anomalies by randomly selecting a feature and split value. Because anomalies require fewer partitions to isolate than normal points, it handles high-dimensional, multi-modal structures effectively without making parametric assumptions or requiring matrix inversions.
